text: Add package-level READMEs for backend, broker, and shared packages
description: Document the role, local runtime behavior, and boundaries of the backend, broker, and shared packages. Keep package docs short but accurate so contributors can navigate the repo without reverse-engineering package purpose.

reflections.context: Add missing package-level docs for the core packages that new contributors hit immediately when reading the repo.
reflections.outcome: Created README files for backend, shared, and broker with concise explanations of purpose, runtime role, commands, and package boundaries.
reflections.effectiveness: High. These were low-risk additions with immediate value for repo readability and open-source onboarding.
reflections.lesson: Package docs are most useful when they explain boundaries and runtime role, not when they try to restate every implementation detail.
reflections.pattern: This mirrors earlier repo-surface cleanup work: small documentation patches often unlock disproportionately better orientation for first-time readers.

status.comment: Added contributor-facing READMEs for the backend, shared package, and wrapped-agents broker. The docs now explain each package’s role, local runtime behavior, commands, and boundaries within the local-first architecture.
